channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Tunable and nonlinearity-enhanced dispersive-plus-dissipative coupling in photon-pressure circuits
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 24 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70459-3

This work demonstrates a nonlinearity-enhanced combination of dispersive and dissipative coupling in photon-pressure circuits, a circuit-QED analog of optomechanics, and reveals how transparency and backaction are modified by coupling-interference. The results hold relevance for radiation-pressure systems, (quantum) control protocols and Hamiltonian simulation.
item.dc:title: Tunable and nonlinearity-enhanced dispersive-plus-dissipative coupling in photon-pressure circuits
item.dc:creator: Mohamad Kazouini
Janis Peter
Zisu Emily Guo
Benedikt Wilde
Kevin Uhl
Dieter Koelle
Reinhold Kleiner
Daniel Bothner

item.title: Emergent discrete space-time crystal of Majorana-like quasiparticles in chiral liquid crystals
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 24 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70880-8

Time crystals, which break time translation symmetry, have garnered significant interest in both quantum and classical systems due to their potential applications and fundamental implications. This study demonstrates the emergence of classical discrete space-time crystals in chiral liquid crystals, revealing the behavior of Majorana-like quasiparticles within facilitating period-doubling phenomena across various conditions.
item.dc:title: Emergent discrete space-time crystal of Majorana-like quasiparticles in chiral liquid crystals
item.dc:creator: Hanqing Zhao
Rui Zhang
Ivan I. Smalyukh

item.title: Crystal symmetry-dependent Orbital Rashba Edelstein effect in epitaxial CuO thin film
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 24 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-71018-6

Epitaxial CuO thin films exhibit crystal-symmetry-dependent orbital Rashba–Edelstein effects, enabling anisotropic orbital torque generation. The work reveals how crystal symmetry governs orbital angular momentum transport in oxide spintronic systems.
item.dc:title: Crystal symmetry-dependent Orbital Rashba Edelstein effect in epitaxial CuO thin film
item.dc:creator: Rui Xiao
Tieyang Zhao
Insu Baek
Hojun Lee
Zhenyi Zheng
Shu Shi
Zhiru Xie
Qihan Zhang
Lanxin Jia
Ping Yang
Xiaojiang Yu
Dongsheng Song
Hyun-Woo Lee
Jingsheng Chen

item.title: Copper-hydroxyl interactions drive water-promoted copper surface oxidation and mobility
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 24 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70947-6

Water and oxygen jointly influence the oxidation of copper, but their respective roles are difficult to disentangle. Here, the authors show that hydroxyl species adsorb at the Cu/CuOx interface, roughen the surface, and enable deeper oxygen penetration and higher Cu mobility.
item.dc:title: Copper-hydroxyl interactions drive water-promoted copper surface oxidation and mobility
item.dc:creator: Shuang Li
Zhongliang Cao
Haolong Chang
Wei Zheng
Guoao Luo
Lifeng Zhang
Weiwei Zhang
Langli Luo
Xing Chen
